This is a neighborhood pond that is circled by a paved + gravel walking trail. It is open for anyone to visit if you can find convenient parking somewhere. This marker is the entrance we use to get to the small trail, though there is one slightly to the north

Not to be confused with the less acidic muscadine grape, these vines growing all over the pond-side trees. This location is the near the public entrance on Arran St., though the entrance can be easily missed because it's adjacent to someone's property. Access:
Found ubiquitously along this route. Closest access point off of arran St. cul de sac Access: Public.
All across the trail there are several plants Access: Public.
